You are a correctness judge for a local Copilot CLI evaluation harness.

Rules:
- Use only the evidence provided in this prompt.
- Be conservative. If evidence is incomplete, say so.
- Judge implementation correctness and task completion first, then completeness and minimality.
- Output exactly one JSON object and nothing else.
- The JSON object must follow this schema exactly:
  {
    "verdict": "pass" | "partial" | "fail",
    "correctnessScore": 0-5,
    "completenessScore": 0-5,
    "minimalityScore": 0-5,
    "confidence": "low" | "medium" | "high",
    "issues": ["short string", ...],
    "strengths": ["short string", ...],
    "summary": "one paragraph"
  }

Evaluation guidance:
- correctnessScore: did the run implement the requested behavior correctly based on the diff and validation evidence?
- completenessScore: how fully did it satisfy the task?
- minimalityScore: did it stay focused and avoid unnecessary changes?
- verdict:
  - pass: strong evidence that the task was completed correctly
  - partial: mixed evidence, incomplete work, or notable uncertainty
  - fail: strong evidence that the task was not completed or is clearly wrong

Task metadata:
{
  "id": "004-review-diversity-audit",
  "title": "Review diversity audit and hardening",
  "category": "review-and-hardening",
  "prompt": "Audit the explicit-review routing path in the model-router extension for edge cases around review diversity and fallback behavior. Focus on cases where a review request could accidentally reuse the last implementation model, stay in the same model family when a distinct family is available, or lose the fallback explanation surfaced in status/debug output. If you find a real issue, implement the smallest robust fix, add lightweight regression coverage without new dependencies, and keep the visible status contract coherent. Update docs only if the user-visible routing contract changes.",
  "judgeHints": [
    "The strongest solution finds a concrete review-diversity or fallback bug instead of rewriting large parts of the router speculatively.",
    "Regression coverage should exercise explicit review routing and same-family or same-model fallback behavior.",
    "Keep the fix focused; documentation updates are only needed if visible review-routing behavior changes."
  ],
  "validation": {
    "requireDiff": true,
    "requiredChangedFiles": [
      ".github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs"
    ],
    "requiredPathPrefixes": [
      "tests/"
    ]
  },
  "timeoutMs": 1200000
}

        "content": "Now let me provide you with a comprehensive summary:\n\n---\n\n## COMPREHENSIVE MODEL-ROUTER SKILL/EXTENSION AUDIT\n\n### FILES OVERVIEW\n\nAll relevant files have been found and read. Here's the complete structure:\n\n**SOURCE CODE FILES:**\n1. `.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs` — SOURCE OF TRUTH\n2. `.github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs` — MAIN EXTENSION LOGIC\n3. `.github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs.new` — MINOR VARIANT (formatting)\n\n**DOCUMENTATION:**\n4. `.github/skills/model-router/SKILL.md` — Skill definition & usage guide\n5. `.github/skills/model-router/references/routing-matrix.md` — Detailed routing reference\n6. `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — Repo-level instructions\n\n**CONFIG & DATA:**\n7. `.github/plugin.json` — Plugin manifest\n8. `eval/config/models.json` — Relative cost weights & model metadata\n9. `eval/config/variants.json` — Evaluation run variants\n\n**TESTS:**\n10. `eval/routing-policy.test.mjs` — Comprehensive test suite\n\n**CI:**\n11. `.github/workflows/eval.yml` — Evaluation harness workflow\n\n**README:**\n12. `README.md` — Project overview\n\n---\n\n## 1. MAIN ROUTING LOGIC (How Models Are Selected)\n\n**File:** `.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs` (418 lines) + `.github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs` (351 lines)\n\n### Model Candidates (Tier-based):\n\n```javascript\nexport const MODEL_CANDIDATES = {\n  economy: [\n    { id: \"claude-haiku-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"gpt-4.1\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"gpt-5-mini\", reasoningEffort: \"low\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.4-mini\", reasoningEffort: \"low\" },\n  ],\n  builder: [\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.2\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.3-codex\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.1-codex\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.1\", reasoningEffort: \"medium\" },\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n  ],\n  reasoning: [\n    { id: \"gpt-5\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.4\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n    { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n    { id: \"claude-opus-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"claude-opus-4.6\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"claude-opus-4.6-1m\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    { id: \"gpt-5.1-codex-max\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n  ],\n};\n```\n\n### Task Classification Logic:\n\nThe `classifyPrompt()` function (lines 246-304 in policy.mjs) classifies prompts into 6 types:\n\n1. **\"review\"** → reasoning tier\n   - Triggers on: \"review\", \"code review\", \"audit\", \"approval readiness\", \"find bugs\", \"regression hunting\"\n   \n2. **\"planning\"** → builder or reasoning (depends on complexity)\n   - Triggers on: \"plan\", \"design\", \"architecture\", \"architect\", \"proposal\", \"strategy\", \"approach\"\n\n3. **\"debugging\"** → builder or reasoning (depends on complexity)\n   - Triggers on: \"debug\", \"fix\", \"failure\", \"error\", \"crash\", \"root cause\", \"broken\", \"investigate\"\n\n4. **\"implementation\"** → complex tier selection based on signals\n   - Default: builder tier\n   - Economy: if includes light keywords (\"explain\", \"summarize\", etc.) AND complexity = 0\n   - Reasoning: if complex orchestration + complexity >= 2, OR complexity >= 4\n   - Triggers on: \"implement\", \"build\", \"create\", \"write\", \"refactor\", \"add\", \"change\", \"update\", \"edit\", \"enhance\", \"modify\", \"adjust\", \"improve\"\n\n5. **\"lightweight\"** → economy tier\n   - Triggers on: \"explain\", \"summarize\", \"what is\", \"where is\", \"find\", \"list\", \"show\", \"search\"\n\n6. **\"general\"** → depends on complexity\n   - Default for anything else\n   - economy if complexity 0-1, builder if complexity 1-2, reasoning if tool-heavy + complexity >= 1 or complex orchestration + complexity >= 2\n\n### Complexity Scoring:\n\nThe `getComplexity()` function (lines 207-244) awards points for:\n- Word count > 40: +1\n- Word count > 100: +1\n- Keywords (\"multi-file\", \"entire codebase\", \"carefully\", \"thorough\", \"complex\"): +2\n- Planning/debugging keywords: +1\n- File path patterns (/.github/, /src/, etc.): +1\n- Tool keywords (bash, curl, api, execute, run, etc.): +1\n\n---\n\n## 2. REVIEW DIVERSITY LOGIC (How Review Model Differs from Implementation)\n\n**Key Code:** `extension.mjs` lines 208-211 + `policy.mjs` lines 354-372\n\n### Session State Tracking:\n\n```javascript\nlet lastImplementationModel = null;  // Tracks last model used for implementation/debugging\nlet lastReviewModel = null;          // Tracks last model used for review\n```\n\n### How Diversity is Enforced:\n\n1. **Recording Implementation Model** (extension.mjs, lines 254-256):\n```javascript\nif (route.kind === \"implementation\" || route.kind === \"debugging\") {\n  lastImplementationModel = chosen.id || current.modelId || lastImplementationModel;\n}\n```\n\n2. **Excluding Implementation Model from Review Candidates** (extension.mjs, lines 208-211):\n```javascript\n// Review diversity: exclude last implementation model even when getCurrent() fails\nif (route.kind === \"review\" && lastImplementationModel) {\n  candidates = candidates.filter((c) => c.id !== lastImplementationModel);\n}\n```\n\n3. **Reordering Candidates by Family Similarity** (policy.mjs, lines 361-372):\n```javascript\nexport function orderCandidates(candidates, route, lastImplModel = null) {\n  if (route.kind !== \"review\" || !lastImplModel) {\n    return candidates;\n  }\n  return [...candidates].sort((left, right) => {\n    const leftSame =\n      left.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(left.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;\n    const rightSame =\n      right.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(right.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;\n    return leftSame - rightSame; // lower \"sameness\" score → earlier in list\n  });\n}\n```\n\nThis **three-tier preference system**:\n- Score 2: NEVER use (same exact model as implementation)\n- Score 1: Deprioritize (same model family, e.g., Claude vs Claude)\n- Score 0: Prioritize (different family, e.g., Claude vs GPT)\n\n### Family Detection (policy.mjs, lines 340-352):\n```javascript\nexport function isSameModelFamily(first, second) {\n  const family = (model) => {\n    if (model.startsWith(\"claude-haiku\")) return \"claude-haiku\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"claude-sonnet\")) return \"claude-sonnet\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"claude-opus\")) return \"claude-opus\";\n    if (model.includes(\"codex\")) return \"codex\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"gpt-5\")) return \"gpt-5\";\n    if (model.startsWith(\"gpt-4.1\")) return \"gpt-4.1\";\n    return model;\n  };\n  return family(first) === family(second);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. FALLBACK LOGIC (When Preferred Model is Unavailable)\n\n**Key Code:** `extension.mjs` lines 182-228\n\n### Candidate Ordering & Fallback Chain:\n\n```javascript\nasync function trySwitchModel(session, route, currentModelId) {\n  // 1. Get tier-appropriate candidates and reorder for review diversity\n  let candidates = orderCandidates(\n    dedupeCandidates(getTierCandidates(route)),\n    route,\n    lastImplementationModel,\n  );\n\n  // 2. For moderate-complexity builder tasks, try same-model reasoning downgrade FIRST\n  if (\n    (route.kind === \"implementation\" || route.kind === \"general\") &&\n    route.tier === \"builder\" &&\n    route.complexity <= 2 &&\n    currentModelId\n  ) {\n    try {\n      await session.rpc.model.switchTo({ modelId: currentModelId, reasoningEffort: \"low\" });\n      return { id: currentModelId, reasoningEffort: \"low\" };\n    } catch {\n      // fall through to full model switching\n    }\n  }\n\n  // 3. Remove current model from candidates\n  if (currentModelId) {\n    candidates = candidates.filter((c) => c.id !== currentModelId);\n  }\n\n  // 4. For review, exclude last implementation model\n  if (route.kind === \"review\" && lastImplementationModel) {\n    candidates = candidates.filter((c) => c.id !== lastImplementationModel);\n  }\n\n  // 5. Try each candidate in order, return first success\n  let selected = null;\n  for (const candidate of candidates) {\n    try {\n      await session.rpc.model.switchTo({\n        modelId: candidate.id,\n        reasoningEffort: candidate.reasoningEffort,\n      });\n      selected = candidate;\n      break;\n    } catch {\n      continue;  // Try next candidate\n    }\n  }\n\n  // 6. Fallback: return current or last attempted\n  return selected || { id: currentModelId || null, reasoningEffort: null };\n}\n```\n\n### Tier Candidate Fallback Order (policy.mjs, lines 306-328):\n\n```javascript\nexport function getTierCandidates(route) {\n  if (route.kind === \"review\") {\n    // Review tries all tiers: reasoning first, then builder, then economy\n    return [\n      ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning,\n      ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder,\n      ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy,\n    ];\n  }\n\n  if (route.kind === \"planning\" || route.kind === \"debugging\") {\n    return route.tier === \"reasoning\"\n      ? [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder]\n      : [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy];\n  }\n\n  if (route.kind === \"implementation\" || route.kind === \"general\") {\n    return route.tier === \"builder\"\n      ? [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy]\n      : [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning];\n  }\n\n  // Default fallback order\n  return [...MODEL_CANDIDATES.economy, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder, ...MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning];\n}\n```\n\n### Confusion-Based Model Swapping (extension.mjs, lines 112-176):\n\nWhen the extension detects looping/confusion, it swaps to an alternate family (Claude → GPT or vice versa):\n\n```javascript\nasync function getAlternateModel(session, currentModelId) {\n  const currentFamily = getModelFamily(currentModelId);\n  const targetFamily = currentFamily === \"claude\" ? \"gpt\" : \"claude\";\n\n  // Find current tier\n  let currentTier = null;\n  for (const [tier, models] of Object.entries(MODEL_CANDIDATES)) {\n    if (models.some((m) => m.id === currentModelId)) {\n      currentTier = tier;\n      break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Try same tier in alternate family\n  const tierCandidates = MODEL_CANDIDATES[currentTier] || [];\n  const candidates = tierCandidates.filter((m) => getModelFamily(m.id) === targetFamily);\n\n  // If tier exhausted, try next tier up\n  if (candidates.length === 0 && currentTier === \"economy\") {\n    return (MODEL_CANDIDATES.builder || []).find((m) => getModelFamily(m.id) === targetFamily) || null;\n  }\n  if (candidates.length === 0 && currentTier === \"builder\") {\n    return (MODEL_CANDIDATES.reasoning || []).find((m) => getModelFamily(m.id) === targetFamily) || null;\n  }\n\n  return candidates[0] || null;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. ROUTING MATRIX / CONFIG (Task-to-Model Mapping)\n\n**File:** `.github/skills/model-router/references/routing-matrix.md` (98 lines) + `eval/config/models.json` (50 lines)\n\n### Task Type Routing Table:\n\n| Task Type | Preferred Tier | Fallback | Models Tried |\n|-----------|---|---|---|\n| Quick Q&A, short explanation, simple search | Economy | Standard builder | Haiku 4.5, gpt-4.1, gpt-5-mini |\n| Small single-file edit | Economy | Standard builder | Same as above |\n| Normal implementation | Standard builder | Economy for trivial, reasoning for tough | Sonnet, gpt-5.x variants |\n| Multi-file implementation | Standard builder | Reasoning | All builders + reasoning |\n| Complex orchestration (parallel agents, chained tools) | Reasoning | Standard builder | All reasoning + builders |\n| Complex plan or architecture | Reasoning | Strongest standard builder | All reasoning models |\n| Debugging with unclear root cause | Reasoning | Strongest standard builder | All reasoning models |\n| **Explicit code review / audit** | **Reasoning using different model** | **Best available different builder** | **All reasoning, then builders (excluding impl model)** |\n\n### Cost-Aware Model Ordering (eval/config/models.json):\n\n```json\n\"models\": {\n  \"claude-haiku-4.5\": { \"family\": \"claude\", \"tier\": \"economy\", \"relativeWeight\": 1.0 },\n  \"gpt-4.1\": { \"family\": \"gpt\", \"tier\": \"economy\", \"relativeWeight\": 1.0 },\n  \"gpt-5-mini\": { \"family\": \"gpt\", \"tier\": \"economy\", \"relativeWeight\": 1.1 },\n  \"gpt-5.4-mini\": { \"family\": \"gpt\", \"tier\": \"economy\", \"relativeWeight\": 1.2 },\n  \n  \"claude-sonnet-4\": { \"family\": \"claude\", \"tier\": \"builder\", \"relativeWeight\": 2.8 },\n  \"claude-sonnet-4.5\": { \"family\": \"claude\", \"tier\": \"builder\", \"relativeWeight\": 3.1 },\n  \"claude-sonnet-4.6\": { \"family\": \"claude\", \"tier\": \"builder\", \"relativeWeight\": 3.5 },\n  \"gpt-5.1\": { \"family\": \"gpt\", \"tier\": \"builder\", \"relativeWeight\": 3.2 },\n  // ... more builders\n  \n  \"gpt-5\": { \"family\": \"gpt\", \"tier\": \"reasoning\", \"relativeWeight\": 5.0 },\n  \"claude-opus-4.6\": { \"family\": \"claude\", \"tier\": \"reasoning\", \"relativeWeight\": 7.0 },\n  // ... more reasoning\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. STATUS/DEBUG OUTPUT (Fallback Explanations Surface)\n\n**File:** `extension.mjs` lines 308-350 (tool: `model_router_status`)\n\n### Tool Handler Output:\n\n```javascript\ntools: [\n  {\n    name: \"model_router_status\",\n    description: \"Show the current model-router decision state and confusion metrics for this Copilot CLI session.\",\n    handler: async () => {\n      const current = await session.rpc.model.getCurrent().catch(() => ({ modelId: undefined }));\n      const isLooping = detectLoopingBehavior();\n      \n      return JSON.stringify({\n        // Quick-glance summary for local debugging\n        debug: {\n          activeModel: current.modelId || null,\n          lastImplModel: lastImplementationModel,\n          looping: isLooping,\n          errorCount: confusionMetrics.errorCount,\n          repeatedPatternCount: confusionMetrics.repeatedPatternCount,\n          turnsSinceLastSwitch: confusionMetrics.turnCount - confusionMetrics.lastSwitchTurn,\n        },\n        currentModel: current.modelId || null,\n        lastImplementationModel,\n        lastReviewModel,\n        lastDecision: {\n          kind: \"review\" | \"implementation\" | \"planning\" | \"debugging\" | \"lightweight\" | \"general\",\n          complexity: number,\n          selectedModelId: string | null,\n          reasoningEffort: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | null,\n        },\n        confusionMetrics: {\n          turnCount: number,\n          recentMessageCount: number,\n          errorCount: number,\n          repeatedPatternCount: number,\n          turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch: number,\n          isLooping: boolean,\n        },\n      });\n    }\n  }\n]\n```\n\n### Additional Context Injected (policy.mjs, lines 374-417):\n\n```javascript\nexport function buildAdditionalContext(decision, currentModelId, lastImplModel = null) {\n  const lines = [\n    \"Model routing policy is active.\",\n    `Current task class: ${decision.kind}.`,\n    `Selected model for this turn: ${decision.selectedModelId || currentModelId || \"unchanged-current-model\"}.`,\n  ];\n\n  if (decision.reasoningEffort) {\n    lines.push(`Reasoning effort: ${decision.reasoningEffort}.`);\n  }\n\n  // REVIEW DIVERSITY EXPLANATION\n  if (decision.kind === \"review\" && lastImplModel) {\n    lines.push(`Prefer review diversity versus implementation model ${lastImplModel}.`);\n    if (decision.selectedModelId === lastImplModel) {\n      lines.push(\n        \"A distinct review model was not available, so the router fell back to the current best option.\"\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (decision.kind === \"lightweight\") {\n    lines.push(\"Keep the solution lean and avoid premium-model depth unless the task expands.\");\n  }\n\n  if ((decision.kind === \"implementation\" || decision.kind === \"general\") && decision.complexity <= 2) {\n    lines.push(\"Inspect only the most relevant file(s). Implement directly without extensive planning.\");\n  }\n\n  if (decision.kind === \"planning\" || decision.kind === \"debugging\") {\n    lines.push(\"Favor strong reasoning and structured analysis before acting.\");\n  }\n\n  return lines.join(\" \");\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. EXISTING TESTS (Routing Logic Tests)\n\n**File:** `eval/routing-policy.test.mjs` (387 lines)\n\n### Test Coverage:\n\n| Category | Tests |\n|----------|-------|\n| **MODEL_CANDIDATES shape** | Has reasoning/builder/economy tiers, every entry has id and reasoningEffort, each tier has ≥1 entry |\n| **MODEL_FAMILIES derivation** | Contains all candidate IDs, claude set only has claude models, gpt set only has gpt models |\n| **getModelFamily()** | Returns correct family for claude/gpt models, null for unknown models |\n| **normalizePrompt()** | Lowercases input, handles null/undefined gracefully |\n| **matchesPattern()** | Matches whole words (not substrings), matches multi-word phrases |\n| **includesAny()** | Returns true if any pattern matches, false otherwise |\n| **isExplicitReviewRequest()** | Detects review keywords, returns false for non-review |\n| **isToolHeavy()** | Detects tool keywords, returns false for simple prompts |\n| **isComplexOrchestration()** | Detects multi-agent patterns, returns false for simple tasks |\n| **getComplexity()** | 0 for short simple, higher for multi-file, higher for long prompts |\n| **classifyPrompt()** | Correctly classifies review/planning/debugging/implementation/lightweight tasks, escalates complex tasks |\n| **getTierCandidates()** | Review includes all tiers, economy-lightweight returns economy-first |\n| **dedupeCandidates()** | Removes exact duplicates, keeps different reasoningEffort as distinct |\n| **isSameModelFamily()** | True for same family, false for different families |\n| **orderCandidates()** | Non-review routes unchanged, review prefers different families, deprioritizes same family |\n| **buildAdditionalContext()** | Includes routing policy header, review diversity note, lightweight notes, planning/debugging notes |\n\n### Example Test:\n\n```javascript\ndescribe(\"orderCandidates\", () => {\n  it(\"prefers same-family last for review when lastImplModel given\", () => {\n    const candidates = [\n      { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n      { id: \"gpt-5.1\", reasoningEffort: null },\n      { id: \"claude-haiku-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    ];\n    // If last impl was claude-sonnet-4.6, the sorted order should put gpt first\n    const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: \"review\" }, \"claude-sonnet-4.6\");\n    assert.equal(result[0].id, \"gpt-5.1\");  // Different family prioritized\n  });\n});\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. .GITHUB/SKILLS/MODEL-ROUTER DIRECTORY & recovery signals\n- `/model_router_status` tool usage guide\n\n**routing-matrix.md (98 lines)** - Contains:\n- Tool calling tier legend (Economy-Tools, Standard-Tools, Reasoning-Tools)\n- Detailed Diversity-of-thought rule for reviews\n- Notes on cost multipliers and fallback behavior\n\n---\n\n## SUMMARY OF KEY AUDIT FINDINGS\n\n### ✅ STRENGTHS:\n\n1. **Single Source of Truth**: `policy.mjs` is explicitly documented as the authoritative source (line 2-10)\n2. **Review Diversity Enforcement**: Three-tier preference system (exact model excluded, same family deprioritized, different family prioritized)\n3. **Comprehensive Fallback Logic**: \n   - Same-model reasoning downgrade attempt first\n   - Try all tier candidates in order\n   - Alternate family swap for confusion detection\n   - Graceful returns when all fail\n4. **Explicit Review Only**: Code explicitly checks `route.kind === \"review\"` before applying diversity logic\n5. **Well-Tested**: 387-line test suite with 16+ describe blocks covering all major functions\n6. **Good Status/Debug Output**: `/model_router_status` tool surfaces decision state and confusion metrics\n7. **Documented Fallback Explanations**: `buildAdditionalContext()` explicitly surfaces when \"distinct review model was not available\"\n\n### ⚠️ AREAS TO AUDIT:\n\n1. **Review Diversity Bug Risk**: Line 209 in extension.mjs filters out `lastImplementationModel` AFTER `getTierCandidates()` and `dedupeCandidates()` but BEFORE `orderCandidates()`. Verify the order is correct.\n\n2. **Confusion Detection Thresholds**: Lines 34-40 set thresholds (minTurnsSinceLastSwitch: 5, consecutiveErrorThreshold: 2, similarityThreshold: 0.75). Consider if these are tuned correctly.\n\n3. **Session State Reset**: Lines 237-240 reset confusion metrics on session start. Verify this doesn't lose important long-session context.\n\n4. **Model Family Detection**: Lines 340-352 use hardcoded prefixes for family detection. So after the stable sort, the test passes with gpt-5.1 first, but in a real review scenario, if gpt-5.1 fails during `trySwitchModel`, we'd fall back to claude-haiku-4.5 since claude-sonnet-4.6 gets filtered out—which means we'd lose provider diversity by staying within Anthropic's models. And claude-haiku-4.5 is definitely available since the economy tier gets included in the real review candidates.\n\nThe solution is to modify the scoring logic in `orderCandidates` to compare at the high-level provider family rather than the specific model subfamily, so we're prioritizing based on whether it's Claude or GPT instead of the exact variant. So the sorting logic is working as intended—GPT models come first since they're a different family, then other Claude models, with the exact match last so it gets filtered out. This gives us the right diversity behavior: prefer GPT for review when the implementation was Claude, but fall back to other Claude variants if needed.\n\nNow I should check whether the test for `orderCandidates` needs to be updated... The test passes with the current scoring, so I need to add a specific test case to verify that claude-haiku gets deprioritized correctly when the last implementation was also a Claude model. I also need to update the `orderCandidates` function to use `getModelFamily` for proper family comparison, and ensure the fallback note displays correctly in `buildAdditionalContext`.\n\nLooking at where `isSameModelFamily` is used, it's only in `orderCandidates` and the test file, so I can safely change the diversity ordering logic without affecting the confusion swap logic elsewhere.\n\nThe fix is to update `orderCandidates` to use `getModelFamily` instead of `isSameModelFamily` for the family comparison, keeping `isSameModelFamily` available for future use where sub-family discrimination matters.\n\nImplementing the fixes now...\n\nUpdating the diversity scoring logic...         "messageId": "203c3ba2-6d46-418b-ba2b-158da24627c7",
        "content": "Found two real bugs. Now implementing fixes:\n\n**Bug 1** — `orderCandidates` uses `isSameModelFamily` (sub-family: haiku/sonnet/opus) rather than `getModelFamily` (provider-level: claude/gpt). This lets `claude-opus` and `claude-haiku` compete with gpt models in the first-priority slot when the impl model was `claude-sonnet`, bypassing true cross-provider diversity.\n\n**Bug 2** — `buildAdditionalContext` only surfaces the fallback explanation when `selectedModelId === lastImplModel`, not when `selectedModelId === null` (getCurrent failed + all switches failed), silently dropping the hint.",
        "toolRequests": [
          {
            "toolCallId": "tooluse_mlyDhN1Le9ZnKm7fhtPnI9",
            "name": "edit",
            "arguments": {
              "new_str": "/**\n * Re-order candidates for review diversity: models from a different provider\n * family (claude vs gpt) than lastImplModel float to the front.\n *\n * Uses getModelFamily (provider-level: \"claude\" | \"gpt\") rather than\n * isSameModelFamily (sub-family: haiku/sonnet/opus) so that claude-opus and\n * claude-haiku are deprioritised alongside claude-sonnet when the last\n * implementation used a claude model — not promoted to the same front-of-list\n * slot as gpt alternatives.\n *\n * Scoring: 0 = different provider family (front) · 1 = same provider family\n * (middle) · 2 = exact model id match (back, and filtered out by trySwitchModel).\n *\n * Accepts lastImplModel as an explicit parameter (rather than reading module\n * state) so the function stays pure and testable.\n */\nexport function orderCandidates(candidates, route, lastImplModel = null) {\n  if (route.kind !== \"review\" || !lastImplModel) {\n    return candidates;\n  }\n  const implFamily = getModelFamily(lastImplModel);\n  const score = (id) => {\n    if (id === lastImplModel) return 2;\n    if (implFamily && getModelFamily(id) === implFamily) return 1;\n    return 0;\n  };\n  return [...candidates].sort((left, right) => score(left.id) - score(right.id));\n}",
              "old_str": "/**\n * Re-order candidates for review diversity: models from a different family\n * than lastImplModel float to the front.\n *\n * Accepts lastImplModel as an explicit parameter (rather than reading module\n * state) so the function stays pure and testable.\n */\nexport function orderCandidates(candidates, route, lastImplModel = null) {\n  if (route.kind !== \"review\" || !lastImplModel) {\n    return candidates;\n  }\n  return [...candidates].sort((left, right) => {\n    const leftSame =\n      left.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(left.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;\n    const rightSame =\n      right.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(right.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;\n    return leftSame - rightSame; // lower \"sameness\" score → earlier in list\n  });\n}",
              "path": "/home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T153138Z/worktrees/004-review-diversity-audit/router/.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs"
            },
            "type": "function",
            "intentionSummary": "edit the file at /home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T153138Z/worktrees/004-review-diversity-audit/router/.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs."
          }
        ],
        "interactionId": "171855f8-66a8-4329-905f-974e46eca2f9",
        "reasoningOpaque": "",
        "reasoningText": "Now I have a clear picture of the bugs. Let me implement the fixes:\n\n**Bug 1**: `orderCandidates` uses `isSameModelFamily` (sub-family: haiku/sonnet/opus) instead of `getModelFamily` (high-level: claude/gpt) for diversity scoring. This means claude-haiku and claude-opus are treated as \"different\" from claude-sonnet and get score 0, competing with gpt models. They should get score 1 (same high-level family, different model).\n\n**Bug 2**: `buildAdditionalContext` only shows the fallback explanation when `selectedModelId === lastImplModel`. \n\nThere's also an edge case where if `selectedModelId` is null because both `getCurrent` and all the fallback switches failed, the fallback note gets dropped entirely—it should trigger in that scenario too. Now I'll implement these fixes and add regression tests to catch them.",

      }

            "arguments": {
              "new_str": "  it(\"prefers same-family last for review when lastImplModel given\", () => {\n    const candidates = [\n      { id: \"claude-sonnet-4.6\", reasoningEffort: \"high\" },\n      { id: \"gpt-5.1\", reasoningEffort: null },\n      { id: \"claude-haiku-4.5\", reasoningEffort: null },\n    ];\n    // If last impl was claude-sonnet-4.6, the sorted order should put gpt first\n    const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: \"review\" }, \"claude-sonnet-4.6\");\n    assert.equal(result[0].id, \"gpt-5.1\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"deprioritises all claude models (not just same assert.ok(pos(\"gpt-5.1\") < pos(\"claude-haiku-4.5\"), \"gpt-5.1 must come before claude-haiku\");\n    assert.ok(pos(\"claude-opus-4.5\") < pos(\"claude-sonnet-4.6\"), \"claude-opus before exact // claude\n      { id: \"gpt-4.1\", reasoningEffort: null },     // gpt, different sub-family\n      { id: \"gpt-5.1\", reasoningEffort: null },     // exact match\n    ];\n    const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: \"review\" }, \"gpt-5.1\");\n    const pos = (id) => result.findIndex((c) => c.id === id);\n    assert.ok(pos(\"claude-sonnet-4.6\") < pos(\"gpt-5.4\"), \"claude before exact match\");\n  });\n});",

            "arguments": {
              "command": "cd /home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T153138Z/worktrees/004-review-diversity-audit/router && git commit -m \"fix: use provider-level family for review diversity, surface null-model fallback\n\nTwo bugs in the explicit-review routing path:\n\n1. orderCandidates used isSameModelFamily (sub-family: haiku/sonnet/opus) to\n   score diversity candidates.  This caused claude-opus and claude-haiku to\n   receive the same score-0 slot as gpt models when the last implementation\n   used a claude-sonnet model, letting them win the review if only gpt-5 and\n   gpt-5.4 were unavailable.  Fix: switch to getModelFamily (provider-level:\n   claude vs gpt) so all claude models score 1 and only cross-provider models\n   score 0 when the impl was claude (and vice-versa).\n\n2. buildAdditionalContext only emitted the fallback explanation line when\n   selectedModelId === lastImplModel.  When getCurrent() fails and all\n   switchTo calls also fail, selectedModelId is null — a clear degraded\n   state — but the hint was silently dropped.  Fix: also emit the note when\n   selectedModelId === null.\n\nAdds five regression tests:\n- orderCandidates: claude-opus/haiku deprioritised vs gpt when impl=claude-sonnet\n- orderCandidates: claude deprioritised vs other-gpt when impl=gpt-5.1\n- buildAdditionalContext: fallback note on selectedModelId === lastImplModel (explicit)\n- buildAdditionalContext: fallback note on selectedModelId === null\n- buildAdditionalContext: no fallback note when a distinct model was successfully selected\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"",
              "description": "Commit fixes"
            },
            "type": "function",
            "intentionSummary": "Commit fixes"
          }
        ],
        "interactionId": "171855f8-66a8-4329-905f-974e46eca2f9",

      "value": {
        "messageId": "480e52b2-06af-46b9-8d11-88b6d5223f38",
        "content": "",
        "toolRequests": [
          {
            "toolCallId": "tooluse_Gj54HJ4xgO6DzD9BN3J12m",
            "name": "task_complete",
            "arguments": {
              "summary": "## Audit results & fixes\n\nTwo real bugs found and fixed in `policy.mjs`, with 5 regression tests added to `eval/routing-policy.test.mjs`. All 56 tests pass (51 pre-existing + 5 new). No doc changes needed — the user-visible routing contract (\"prefer a different model for review than implementation\") is unchanged.\n\n---\n\n### Bug 1 — `orderCandidates`: sub-family comparison used instead of provider-level family\n\n**Root cause**: The sort scored diversity candidates using `isSameModelFamily`, which discriminates at the sub-family level (haiku / sonnet / opus). This gave `claude-opus` and `claude-haiku` the same score-0 (front-of-list) slot as gpt models when the last implementation used `claude-sonnet-4.6`. If `gpt-5` and `gpt-5.4` were both unavailable, the next candidate tried was `claude-opus-4.5` — still an Anthropic model, defeating the cross-provider diversity goal.\n\n**Fix**: Replace `isSameModelFamily` with `getModelFamily` (which returns `\"claude\"` | `\"gpt\"`) in the scoring lambda inside `orderCandidates`. Now all claude models receive score 1 when the impl was any claude model, and all gpt models receive score 0 (and vice-versa). `isSameModelFamily` itself is untouched.\n\n---\n\n### Bug 2 — `buildAdditionalContext`: fallback note dropped when `selectedModelId === null`\n\n**Root cause**: The fallback explanation (\"A distinct review model was not available…\") was only emitted when `decision.selectedModelId === lastImplModel`. When `getCurrent()` fails *and* all `switchTo` calls also fail, `selectedModelId` is `null` — a clearly degraded state — but the context hint was silently dropped, leaving the agent with no indication that diversity wasn't achieved.\n\n**Fix**: Also emit the fallback line when `selectedModelId === null`."
            },
            "type": "function",
            "toolTitle": "Task complete",
            "intentionSummary": "## Audit results & fixes\n\nTwo real bugs found and fixed i..."
          }
        ],

diff --git a/.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs b/.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs
index d1fd2e9..554425c 100644
--- a/.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs
+++ b/.github/extensions/model-router/policy.mjs
@@ -352,8 +352,17 @@ export function isSameModelFamily(first, second) {
 }
 
 /**
- * Re-order candidates for review diversity: models from a different family
- * than lastImplModel float to the front.
+ * Re-order candidates for review diversity: models from a different provider
+ * family (claude vs gpt) than lastImplModel float to the front.
+ *
+ * Uses getModelFamily (provider-level: "claude" | "gpt") rather than
+ * isSameModelFamily (sub-family: haiku/sonnet/opus) so that claude-opus and
+ * claude-haiku are deprioritised alongside claude-sonnet when the last
+ * implementation used a claude model — not promoted to the same front-of-list
+ * slot as gpt alternatives.
+ *
+ * Scoring: 0 = different provider family (front) · 1 = same provider family
+ * (middle) · 2 = exact model id match (back, and filtered out by trySwitchModel).
  *
  * Accepts lastImplModel as an explicit parameter (rather than reading module
  * state) so the function stays pure and testable.
@@ -362,13 +371,13 @@ export function orderCandidates(candidates, route, lastImplModel = null) {
   if (route.kind !== "review" || !lastImplModel) {
     return candidates;
   }
-  return [...candidates].sort((left, right) => {
-    const leftSame =
-      left.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(left.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;
-    const rightSame =
-      right.id === lastImplModel ? 2 : isSameModelFamily(right.id, lastImplModel) ? 1 : 0;
-    return leftSame - rightSame; // lower "sameness" score → earlier in list
-  });
+  const implFamily = getModelFamily(lastImplModel);
+  const score = (id) => {
+    if (id === lastImplModel) return 2;
+    if (implFamily && getModelFamily(id) === implFamily) return 1;
+    return 0;
+  };
+  return [...candidates].sort((left, right) => score(left.id) - score(right.id));
 }
 
 /**
@@ -389,7 +398,7 @@ export function buildAdditionalContext(decision, currentModelId, lastImplModel =
 
   if (decision.kind === "review" && lastImplModel) {
     lines.push(`Prefer review diversity versus implementation model ${lastImplModel}.`);
-    if (decision.selectedModelId === lastImplModel) {
+    if (decision.selectedModelId === lastImplModel || decision.selectedModelId === null) {
       lines.push(
         "A distinct review model was not available, so the router fell back to the current best option.",
       );
diff --git a/eval/routing-policy.test.mjs b/eval/routing-policy.test.mjs
index fdb892a..5be8bce 100644
--- a/eval/routing-policy.test.mjs
+++ b/eval/routing-policy.test.mjs
@@ -348,6 +348,39 @@ describe("orderCandidates", () => {
     const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: "review" }, "claude-sonnet-4.6");
     assert.equal(result[0].id, "gpt-5.1");
   });
+
+  it("deprioritises all claude models (not just same sub-family) when impl was claude-sonnet", () => {
+    // Regression: isSameModelFamily classified claude-opus/haiku as "different"
+    // from claude-sonnet, giving them the same score-0 priority slot as gpt.
+    // After the fix, getModelFamily is used so all claude models score 1 and
+    // only gpt models score 0.
+    const candidates = [
+      { id: "claude-opus-4.5", reasoningEffort: null },   // claude, different sub-family
+      { id: "gpt-5.1", reasoningEffort: null },            // gpt
+      { id: "claude-haiku-4.5", reasoningEffort: null },   // claude, different sub-family
+      { id: "claude-sonnet-4.6", reasoningEffort: "high" },// exact match
+    ];
+    const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: "review" }, "claude-sonnet-4.6");
+    const pos = (id) => result.findIndex((c) => c.id === id);
+    assert.ok(pos("gpt-5.1") < pos("claude-opus-4.5"), "gpt-5.1 must come before claude-opus");
+    assert.ok(pos("gpt-5.1") < pos("claude-haiku-4.5"), "gpt-5.1 must come before claude-haiku");
+    assert.ok(pos("claude-opus-4.5") < pos("claude-sonnet-4.6"), "claude-opus before exact match");
+    assert.ok(pos("claude-haiku-4.5") < pos("claude-sonnet-4.6"), "claude-haiku before exact match");
+  });
+
+  it("deprioritises all gpt models (not just same sub-family) when impl was gpt-5.1", () => {
+    const candidates = [
+      { id: "gpt-5.4", reasoningEffort: null },     // gpt, different sub-family
+      { id: "claude-sonnet-4.6", reasoningEffort: null }, // claude
+      { id: "gpt-4.1", reasoningEffort: null },     // gpt, different sub-family
+      { id: "gpt-5.1", reasoningEffort: null },     // exact match
+    ];
+    const result = orderCandidates(candidates, { kind: "review" }, "gpt-5.1");
+    const pos = (id) => result.findIndex((c) => c.id === id);
+    assert.ok(pos("claude-sonnet-4.6") < pos("gpt-5.4"), "claude must come before other gpt");
+    assert.ok(pos("claude-sonnet-4.6") < pos("gpt-4.1"), "claude must come before other gpt");
+    assert.ok(pos("gpt-5.4") < pos("gpt-5.1"), "other gpt before exact match");
+  });
 });
 
 // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@@ -383,4 +416,26 @@ describe("buildAdditionalContext", () => {
     const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, "claude-sonnet-4.6", null);
     assert.ok(ctx.includes("reasoning"));
   });
+
+  it("includes fallback note when selectedModelId matches lastImplModel", () => {
+    const decision = { kind: "review", complexity: 1, selectedModelId: "claude-sonnet-4.6", reasoningEffort: null };
+    const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, "claude-sonnet-4.6", "claude-sonnet-4.6");
+    assert.ok(ctx.includes("fell back"));
+  });
+
+  it("includes fallback note when selectedModelId is null and lastImplModel is set", () => {
+    // Regression: when getCurrent() fails AND all switchTo calls fail, selectedModelId
+    // is null. The fallback explanation must still be surfaced so the agent is aware.
+    const decision = { kind: "review", complexity: 1, selectedModelId: null, reasoningEffort: null };
+    const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, null, "claude-sonnet-4.6");
+    assert.ok(ctx.includes("Prefer review diversity"));
+    assert.ok(ctx.includes("fell back"));
+  });
+
+  it("does not include fallback note for review when selectedModelId differs from lastImplModel", () => {
+    const decision = { kind: "review", complexity: 1, selectedModelId: "gpt-5.1", reasoningEffort: null };
+    const ctx = buildAdditionalContext(decision, "gpt-5.1", "claude-sonnet-4.6");
+    assert.ok(ctx.includes("Prefer review diversity"));
+    assert.ok(!ctx.includes("fell back"));
+  });
 });
